description:
You are the on-call Kubernetes operator.  
A colleague just pushed three manifest files to  
/home/user/k8s/manifests  

    deployment.yaml  
    service.yaml  
    configmap.yaml  

Your job is to create a **repeatable, timestamped backup** of those manifests and record the action in a log that an automated audit system will later consume.

Detailed requirements:

1. Use the fixed timestamp 20240102-150000 (10 January 02 15:00:00) for every artefact created in this exercise.  
   • Directory names, archive names, and the log entry must all embed that exact string.  
   • The ISO-8601 time in the log must be 2024-01-02T15:00:00Z.

2. Build the directory structure shown below (create anything that is missing):
   /home/user/backup/20240102-150000        ← copy of the manifests goes here  
   /home/user/backup/archives               ← parent directory for the tarball  
   /home/user/backup/backup.log             ← audit log (append one line)

3. Copy every file that is currently present inside /home/user/k8s/manifests  
   into /home/user/backup/20240102-150000, preserving the original filenames.

4. Pack the **files themselves (not the directory)** into a gzip-compressed
   archive named exactly  
   /home/user/backup/archives/k8s_backup_20240102-150000.tar.gz  
   Requirements for the archive:
   • It must contain only the three manifest files at the archive root  
     (paths inside the archive: deployment.yaml, service.yaml, configmap.yaml).  
   • No parent directories such as “k8s” or “20240102-150000” should appear inside the tarball.

5. Immediately after creating the archive, calculate its SHA-256 checksum.

6. Append a single new line to /home/user/backup/backup.log using **pipe-separated
   fields** in this exact order, with **no spaces** between the pipes:

   TIMESTAMP|ARCHIVE_PATH|FILES|SHA256_CHECKSUM

   For this task the line must therefore look like:

   2024-01-02T15:00:00Z|/home/user/backup/archives/k8s_backup_20240102-150000.tar.gz|3|<checksum>

   • Replace <checksum> with the real 64-character hexadecimal digest you computed.  
   • Do not add extra whitespace, trailing pipes, or additional text.

7. Once finished, print the calculated checksum to STDOUT so the requester can
   see it immediately.

Notes & constraints:

• You do NOT need root privileges; everything must stay inside /home/user.  
• Only the three manifest files currently present in /home/user/k8s/manifests
  should appear in the backup.  
• The log file must stay append-only: leave any earlier lines untouched if the
  file already exists.  
• The automated verification will:  
  – check that the new directory, archive, and log entry exist,  
  – extract the archive to confirm it contains exactly three files at the root,  
  – recompute the SHA-256 digest and compare it with the value in the log.  
• Keep your command sequence concise—about 5 – 10 commands is typical.

When everything is done correctly you should have:

/home/user/backup/20240102-150000/deployment.yaml  
/home/user/backup/20240102-150000/service.yaml  
/home/user/backup/20240102-150000/configmap.yaml  

/home/user/backup/archives/k8s_backup_20240102-150000.tar.gz  

And the last line of /home/user/backup/backup.log identical in structure to the
example shown above, with the real checksum in place of <checksum>.

Do not show the individual commands here—just execute them in the terminal.
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied to the container:

1. Directory /home/user/k8s/manifests containing exactly three files:
   a. /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml
      Content:
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
        name: nginx
      spec:
        replicas: 2

   b. /home/user/k8s/manifests/service.yaml
      Content:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
        name: nginx
      spec:
        type: ClusterIP

   c. /home/user/k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml
      Content:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ConfigMap
      metadata:
        name: nginx-config
      data:
        key: value

2. Directory /home/user/backup exists and is writable by the user; 
   /home/user/backup/archives and /home/user/backup/backup.log may or may not
   exist (tests will create them if absent so the agent can append).

Verification logic used by automated tests:

A. Confirm directory /home/user/backup/20240102-150000 exists and contains
   three files whose SHA-256 checksums match those of the source manifests.

B. Confirm file /home/user/backup/archives/k8s_backup_20240102-150000.tar.gz
   exists and, when extracted, yields exactly:
      deployment.yaml
      service.yaml
      configmap.yaml
   with byte-for-byte content identical to the originals.

C. Parse /home/user/backup/backup.log, read the **last** line, split on “|”,
   and verify:
      field[0] == "2024-01-02T15:00:00Z"
      field[1] == "/home/user/backup/archives/k8s_backup_20240102-150000.tar.gz"
      field[2] == "3"
      field[3] (64-hex-char checksum) equals sha256sum( field[1] )

D. Ensure the stdout of the agent’s final command contains the same checksum
   string that appears in field[3].

Because the checksum is derived at runtime, the test script calculates the
expected digest rather than storing it here.
